output:
CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Current level: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

content.text:
Invoke the `handoff` skill.

The helper scripts ship inside the plugin, NOT the user's project. Resolve them from the plugin root and reuse this as `$SCR` in the skill's commands (`handoff-dir.sh`, `handoff-validate.py`, `handoff-render.py`, `handoff_paths.py`):

```
$HOME/.claude/plugins/cache/catalyst/catalyst/0.5.47/scripts
```

If the user passed a single recognized keyword as `$ARGUMENT`, route to that mode:

- `read` or `resume` → READ mode (load existing brief for current branch / key)
- `recover` or `rebuild` → RECOVER mode (rebuild a degraded brief)
- `reground` → REGROUND mode (read-only re-injection of the current key's load-bearing fields: goal, locked decisions, files to keep in view — no disk write, no mismatch checks)
- `split` → SPLIT mode (fork the braided session into N self-contained briefs: analyze → propose threads → user confirms → write one validated brief per thread + one combined PROJECT_STATE fork entry → print resume prompts)
- any other non-empty value → WRITE mode with `$ARGUMENT` as the tier-1 explicit key (writes the validated JSON brief to `<store>/<argument>.json`)
- empty argument → WRITE mode, resolve key via the ladder (branch → legacy)

Then follow the skill's procedure for the selected mode exactly. Confirm to the user which mode + which key was used.


ARGUMENTS: read catalyst-dogfood-build

input.command: SCR=$HOME/.claude/plugins/cache/catalyst/catalyst/0.5.47/scripts; python3 "$SCR/handoff-render.py" catalyst-dogfood-build 2>&1 || echo "---RENDER FAILED, reading raw---";

content.content:
1	# Handoff — 2026-05-24T15:25:00Z
2	# Key: catalyst-dogfood-build
3	
4	## Resume prompt
5	> read .claude/handoffs/catalyst-dogfood-build.md and continue. next acceptance check: build Catalyst Tier 2 (evaluator-library + failure-pattern-detector + pipeline-templates) using Catalyst's own skills as the harness.
6	
7	## Re-entry instructions
8	- Resume by: launching a fresh session, invoking `catalyst:handoff resume`, then starting Tier 2 implementation via `catalyst:pipeline` orchestration of the plan.
9	- Done when: Tier 2 v0.5 released (auto-bump tag visible at github.com/adrijshikhar/catalyst/releases), all 3 skills' evals at pass@3 ≥ 0.90, handoff v0.3 + Tier 1 evals still pass in regression.
10	- Read `.claude/PROJECT_STATE.md` ONLY if you need session-by-session history. The brief below is the entry point.
11	
12	## State packet
13	- **Branch:** main (Catalyst ships from main, auto-release on push)
14	- **Working tree:** clean
15	- **Latest release:** [v0.1.16](https://github.com/adrijshikhar/catalyst/releases/tag/v0.1.16) (2026-05-24T15:23:33Z)
16	- **Latest commit:** `3ced9b4` — fix(handoff): always print Resume prompt verbatim in WRITE/RECOVER confirm
17	- **Tests run this session:**
18	  - All hook smoke tests passing (verify-gate block/allow/stale; 4 lifecycle hooks)
19	  - **eval-13 `fresh-session-resumes-from-brief` ran live: 9/9 (100%)** — handoff skill PASSED real-world dogfood test. Subagent given this brief Read it first, surfaced decisions/rejected paths verbatim, followed dogfood plan order, did NOT auto-read PROJECT_STATE.md.
20	  - Lint OK after every commit.
21	- **Commands that mattered:** `gh release list`, `bash scripts/install-hooks.sh install ...`, `python3 scripts/lint.py`, `git pull --rebase`, `gh run list -R adrijshikhar/catalyst`
22	- **Decisions affecting next session:**
23	  - **Tier 1 SHIPPED + real-world-validated.** v0.4 live as v0.1.15. handoff skill scored 9/9 on eval-13 dogfood test. Treat the resume contract as load-bearing.
24	  - **Next build target: Tier 2** per [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md](../../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md). 3 skills: `evaluator-library` (6 domain rubrics: code-quality, ui-design, prose, security, performance, accessibility), `failure-pattern-detector` (Stop hook + 6 OpenDev patterns), `pipeline-templates` (`/pipeline run <name>` + 3 bundled templates).
25	  - **Dogfood mandate:** Build Tier 2 USING `catalyst:handoff` PIPELINE mode, `catalyst:verify-gate` (evidence gates), `catalyst:hook-builder install --all` (ambient mode). Use `superpowers:writing-plans` to expand the Tier 2 spec into per-task plan, then `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` to execute.
26	  - **EDD ordering enforced by commit sequence.** `test(scope):` commits land BEFORE `feat(scope):` for same scope. Verified by commit log on Tier 1.
27	  - **POSIX bash + jq only for hooks** (no Python deps). Established in Tier 1.
28	  - **Anti-self-grade is HARD RULE.** Evaluator subagent in `evaluator-library` MUST NEVER see the generator's transcript. Add a dedicated eval to every Tier 2 skill that uses an evaluator role.
29	  - **Version policy:** auto-bump patch on every push. For Tier 2 milestone, manually edit `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` to `0.5.0` BEFORE the push that ships Tier 2 (otherwise milestone version is lost).
30	  - **Eval-13 now part of release-gate regression bar.** Any future handoff change must keep eval-13 at 9/9.
31	- **Rejected paths (do NOT re-litigate):**
32	  - **Tool-output compactor** — rtk + caveman cover this layer at user tooling level. Removed from Tier 1 scope mid-session.
33	  - **Catalyst as Company Brain competitor** — saturated ecosystem (gbrain, brAIn, ContextBrain, codebase-memory-mcp, etc.). Bridge via Tier 3 `brain-bridge` instead.
34	  - **Generic harness scaffolder** — Harness Engineer plugin already ships init/doctor/gc/onboard. Don't compete.
35	  - **Autonomous-loop / `/goal` replacement** — Claude Code platform owns this; wrong shape for Catalyst's collaborative-first positioning.
36	  - **`claude -p` headless integration eval for Option B** — DEFERRED to v0.7+ per [docs/ROADMAP.md Future-work index](../../docs/ROADMAP.md). Need ANTHROPIC_API_KEY CI secret + Tier 5+ `dogfood-eval` skill.
37	  - **Catalyst-patterns as shipped skill** — that's `CLAUDE.md` content, not a plugin skill. Hard rule.
38	- **Open risks:**
39	  - Tier 2 `failure-pattern-detector` overlaps with Tier 3 `session-degradation-watch` on repeated-tool-call + stale-read. Boundary decision: T2 = end-of-session via Stop hook, T3 = real-time via UserPromptSubmit. Verify in implementation.
40	  - Plugin marketplace supply-chain risk (ClawHavoc Jan 2026, 1200 malicious skills). Mitigated by small surface area, no MCP server, no network in hooks. Tier 4 `skill-supply-chain-audit` is the durable fix.
41	  - Auto-bump release pipeline has glitched once on push trigger (v0.1.7 episode). Manual `gh workflow run` is escape hatch.
42	  - macOS `date -j` vs Linux `date -d` in verify-gate hook — handled, but Windows native unsupported (POSIX bash + jq only).
43	- **Next acceptance check:** Tier 2 v0.5 shipped with all 3 skills passing pass@3 ≥ 0.90 + handoff v0.3 / Tier 1 / eval-13 regression at pass^3 = 1.00.
44	
45	## Files to read first
46	- [docs/ROADMAP.md](../../docs/ROADMAP.md) — 10-skill roadmap, current status, design principles, what-NOT-to-build, Future-work index
47	- [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md](../../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md) — Tier 2 spec (next target)
48	- [CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md) — repo conventions (commits, EDD ordering, skill layout, hook authoring)
49	- [docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-tier-1-harness-primitives.md](../../docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-tier-1-harness-primitives.md) — Tier 1 implementation plan (template for Tier 2 plan structure)
50	- [skills/handoff/SKILL.md](../../skills/handoff/SKILL.md), [skills/verify-gate/SKILL.md](../../skills/verify-gate/SKILL.md), [skills/hook-builder/SKILL.md](../../skills/hook-builder/SKILL.md) — the 3 shipped skills to dogfood
51	- [skills/handoff/evals/evals.json](../../skills/handoff/evals/evals.json) — eval-13 lives here; treat as regression bar
52	- [skills/handoff/evals/evals.log](../../skills/handoff/evals/evals.log) — 2 entries: v0.3 iter-1 + v0.4 iter-2 (today's 9/9 dogfood)
53	
54	## Files to NOT load by default
55	- `.claude/PROJECT_STATE.md` — historical narrative; this brief names the binding decisions
56	- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-3-knowledge-integration-design.md` — Tier 3 spec; not relevant for Tier 2 build
57	- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-4-safety-coordination-design.md` — Tier 4 spec; not relevant for Tier 2 build
58	- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-pipeline-skill-design.md` — SUPERSEDED by handoff v0.3 spec; do not implement from it
59	- `skills/handoff-workspace/`, eval fixtures dirs — gitignored, no content on fresh clone
60	
61	## Dogfood plan for next session
62	
63	1. `catalyst:handoff resume` — load this brief
64	2. `catalyst:hook-builder install --all` — wire ambient mode for Tier 2 build (PreCompact + SessionStart + Stop + UserPromptSubmit)
65	3. `catalyst:verify-gate install` — enforce evidence-first writes during Tier 2 implementation (block claim-success without prior Read)
66	4. `superpowers:writing-plans` — expand Tier 2 spec into per-task implementation plan (mirror the Tier 1 plan structure)
67	5. `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` — execute Tier 2 plan task-by-task with two-stage reviews (spec compliance → code quality)
68	6. After all tasks complete: edit `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` version to `0.5.0` for milestone, commit, push → auto-release fires
69	7. Verify eval-13 still 9/9 after Tier 2 changes (regression bar)
70	8. `catalyst:handoff` (no arg) — write a new brief for Tier 3 work; this brief retires
71	
72	This is THE canonical use case for Catalyst — multi-week feature with explicit per-stage checks, generated by the very tools being shipped. Document the experience in PROJECT_STATE.md after Tier 2 ships.
73

# Handoff — 2026-05-24T15:25:00Z
# Key: catalyst-dogfood-build

## Resume prompt
> read .claude/handoffs/catalyst-dogfood-build.md and continue. next acceptance check: build Catalyst Tier 2 (evaluator-library + failure-pattern-detector + pipeline-templates) using Catalyst's own skills as the harness.

## Re-entry instructions
- Resume by: launching a fresh session, invoking `catalyst:handoff resume`, then starting Tier 2 implementation via `catalyst:pipeline` orchestration of the plan.
- Done when: Tier 2 v0.5 released (auto-bump tag visible at github.com/adrijshikhar/catalyst/releases), all 3 skills' evals at pass@3 ≥ 0.90, handoff v0.3 + Tier 1 evals still pass in regression.
- Read `.claude/PROJECT_STATE.md` ONLY if you need session-by-session history. The brief below is the entry point.

## State packet
- **Branch:** main (Catalyst ships from main, auto-release on push)
- **Working tree:** clean
- **Latest release:** [v0.1.16](https://github.com/adrijshikhar/catalyst/releases/tag/v0.1.16) (2026-05-24T15:23:33Z)
- **Latest commit:** `3ced9b4` — fix(handoff): always print Resume prompt verbatim in WRITE/RECOVER confirm
- **Tests run this session:**
  - All hook smoke tests passing (verify-gate block/allow/stale; 4 lifecycle hooks)
  - **eval-13 `fresh-session-resumes-from-brief` ran live: 9/9 (100%)** — handoff skill PASSED real-world dogfood test. Subagent given this brief Read it first, surfaced decisions/rejected paths verbatim, followed dogfood plan order, did NOT auto-read PROJECT_STATE.md.
  - Lint OK after every commit.
- **Commands that mattered:** `gh release list`, `bash scripts/install-hooks.sh install ...`, `python3 scripts/lint.py`, `git pull --rebase`, `gh run list -R adrijshikhar/catalyst`
- **Decisions affecting next session:**
  - **Tier 1 SHIPPED + real-world-validated.** v0.4 live as v0.1.15. handoff skill scored 9/9 on eval-13 dogfood test. Treat the resume contract as load-bearing.
  - **Next build target: Tier 2** per [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md](../../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md). 3 skills: `evaluator-library` (6 domain rubrics: code-quality, ui-design, prose, security, performance, accessibility), `failure-pattern-detector` (Stop hook + 6 OpenDev patterns), `pipeline-templates` (`/pipeline run <name>` + 3 bundled templates).
  - **Dogfood mandate:** Build Tier 2 USING `catalyst:handoff` PIPELINE mode, `catalyst:verify-gate` (evidence gates), `catalyst:hook-builder install --all` (ambient mode). Use `superpowers:writing-plans` to expand the Tier 2 spec into per-task plan, then `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` to execute.
  - **EDD ordering enforced by commit sequence.** `test(scope):` commits land BEFORE `feat(scope):` for same scope. Verified by commit log on Tier 1.
  - **POSIX bash + jq only for hooks** (no Python deps). Established in Tier 1.
  - **Anti-self-grade is HARD RULE.** Evaluator subagent in `evaluator-library` MUST NEVER see the generator's transcript. Add a dedicated eval to every Tier 2 skill that uses an evaluator role.
  - **Version policy:** auto-bump patch on every push. For Tier 2 milestone, manually edit `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` to `0.5.0` BEFORE the push that ships Tier 2 (otherwise milestone version is lost).
  - **Eval-13 now part of release-gate regression bar.** Any future handoff change must keep eval-13 at 9/9.
- **Rejected paths (do NOT re-litigate):**
  - **Tool-output compactor** — rtk + caveman cover this layer at user tooling level. Removed from Tier 1 scope mid-session.
  - **Catalyst as Company Brain competitor** — saturated ecosystem (gbrain, brAIn, ContextBrain, codebase-memory-mcp, etc.). Bridge via Tier 3 `brain-bridge` instead.
  - **Generic harness scaffolder** — Harness Engineer plugin already ships init/doctor/gc/onboard. Don't compete.
  - **Autonomous-loop / `/goal` replacement** — Claude Code platform owns this; wrong shape for Catalyst's collaborative-first positioning.
  - **`claude -p` headless integration eval for Option B** — DEFERRED to v0.7+ per [docs/ROADMAP.md Future-work index](../../docs/ROADMAP.md). Need ANTHROPIC_API_KEY CI secret + Tier 5+ `dogfood-eval` skill.
  - **Catalyst-patterns as shipped skill** — that's `CLAUDE.md` content, not a plugin skill. Hard rule.
- **Open risks:**
  - Tier 2 `failure-pattern-detector` overlaps with Tier 3 `session-degradation-watch` on repeated-tool-call + stale-read. Boundary decision: T2 = end-of-session via Stop hook, T3 = real-time via UserPromptSubmit. Verify in implementation.
  - Plugin marketplace supply-chain risk (ClawHavoc Jan 2026, 1200 malicious skills). Mitigated by small surface area, no MCP server, no network in hooks. Tier 4 `skill-supply-chain-audit` is the durable fix.
  - Auto-bump release pipeline has glitched once on push trigger (v0.1.7 episode). Manual `gh workflow run` is escape hatch.
  - macOS `date -j` vs Linux `date -d` in verify-gate hook — handled, but Windows native unsupported (POSIX bash + jq only).
- **Next acceptance check:** Tier 2 v0.5 shipped with all 3 skills passing pass@3 ≥ 0.90 + handoff v0.3 / Tier 1 / eval-13 regression at pass^3 = 1.00.

## Files to read first
- [docs/ROADMAP.md](../../docs/ROADMAP.md) — 10-skill roadmap, current status, design principles, what-NOT-to-build, Future-work index
- [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md](../../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-2-orchestration-extensions-design.md) — Tier 2 spec (next target)
- [CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md) — repo conventions (commits, EDD ordering, skill layout, hook authoring)
- [docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-tier-1-harness-primitives.md](../../docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-tier-1-harness-primitives.md) — Tier 1 implementation plan (template for Tier 2 plan structure)
- [skills/handoff/SKILL.md](../../skills/handoff/SKILL.md), [skills/verify-gate/SKILL.md](../../skills/verify-gate/SKILL.md), [skills/hook-builder/SKILL.md](../../skills/hook-builder/SKILL.md) — the 3 shipped skills to dogfood
- [skills/handoff/evals/evals.json](../../skills/handoff/evals/evals.json) — eval-13 lives here; treat as regression bar
- [skills/handoff/evals/evals.log](../../skills/handoff/evals/evals.log) — 2 entries: v0.3 iter-1 + v0.4 iter-2 (today's 9/9 dogfood)

## Files to NOT load by default
- `.claude/PROJECT_STATE.md` — historical narrative; this brief names the binding decisions
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-3-knowledge-integration-design.md` — Tier 3 spec; not relevant for Tier 2 build
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-tier-4-safety-coordination-design.md` — Tier 4 spec; not relevant for Tier 2 build
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-pipeline-skill-design.md` — SUPERSEDED by handoff v0.3 spec; do not implement from it
- `skills/handoff-workspace/`, eval fixtures dirs — gitignored, no content on fresh clone

## Dogfood plan for next session

1. `catalyst:handoff resume` — load this brief
2. `catalyst:hook-builder install --all` — wire ambient mode for Tier 2 build (PreCompact + SessionStart + Stop + UserPromptSubmit)
3. `catalyst:verify-gate install` — enforce evidence-first writes during Tier 2 implementation (block claim-success without prior Read)
4. `superpowers:writing-plans` — expand Tier 2 spec into per-task implementation plan (mirror the Tier 1 plan structure)
5. `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` — execute Tier 2 plan task-by-task with two-stage reviews (spec compliance → code quality)
6. After all tasks complete: edit `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` version to `0.5.0` for milestone, commit, push → auto-release fires
7. Verify eval-13 still 9/9 after Tier 2 changes (regression bar)
8. `catalyst:handoff` (no arg) — write a new brief for Tier 3 work; this brief retires

This is THE canonical use case for Catalyst — multi-week feature with explicit per-stage checks, generated by the very tools being shipped. Document the experience in PROJECT_STATE.md after Tier 2 ships.

The immediate next action (dogfood steps 2–4) needs the Tier 2 spec to expand into a plan. Relevant entries:

---

## (a) Next acceptance check — verbatim
> Tier 2 v0.5 shipped with all 3 skills passing pass@3 ≥ 0.90 + handoff v0.3 / Tier 1 / eval-13 regression at pass^3 = 1.00.

## (b) Top 3 decisions still in force (from state packet)
1. **Tier 1 SHIPPED + real-world-validated.** v0.4 live as v0.1.15; handoff skill scored 9/9 on eval-13 dogfood test. Treat the resume contract as load-bearing.
2. **Next build target: Tier 2** per the Tier 2 design spec — 3 skills: `evaluator-library` (6 domain rubrics), `failure-pattern-detector` (Stop hook + 6 OpenDev patterns), `pipeline-templates` (`/pipeline run <name>` + 3 bundled templates).
3. **Dogfood mandate:** build Tier 2 USING `catalyst:handoff` PIPELINE mode, `catalyst:verify-gate` evidence gates, and `catalyst:hook-builder install --all` (ambient mode); expand spec via `superpowers:writing-plans`, execute via `superpowers:subagent-driven-development`.

(Also still binding: anti-self-grade HARD RULE; POSIX bash + jq only for hooks; manually set `plugin.json` to `0.5.0` before the Tier 2 ship push; eval-13 now in the release-gate regression bar.)

## (c) One rejected path — do NOT re-litigate
**Tool-output compactor** — rtk + caveman already cover that layer at the user-tooling level. Removed from Tier 1 scope mid-session.

3. `catalyst:verify-gate install` — enforce evidence-first writes during Tier 2 implementation
